code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    predicate_templates = {
        "cityServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway number of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fifth runway number of {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the ICAO identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the IATA identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the location identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is natively known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by the {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"the capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses the {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"the official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"the largest city of {s} is {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda s, o: f"the county seat of {s} is {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the administrative arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"the mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway surface of {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the city of {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda s, o: f"{s} has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people from {s} are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates the {o} helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as a transport aircraft",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"the currency of {s} is the {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"{s} is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the class {o}",
        "division": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the division {o}",
        "order": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the order {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"the leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as its hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates the {o} fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses the {o} as an attack aircraft",
        "battle": lambda s, o: f"{s} participated in the {o}",
        "chief": lambda s, o: f"the chief of {s} is {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_predicates = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        subject_predicates[triple.subject].append(triple)

    # Find the main subject (likely the airport)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    # Try to find airport subject first
    for subj in subject_predicates:
        if "airport" in subj.lower() or "Airport" in subj:
            main_subject = subj
            break

    parts = []
    used_subjects = set()

    def process_triple(triple):
        pred = triple.predicate
        subj = triple.subject
        obj = triple.object
        if pred in predicate_templates:
            return predicate_templates[pred](subj, obj)
        else:
            return f"the {pred} of {subj} is {obj}"

    # Build sentence from all triples
    sentences = []
    for triple in triples:
        sentences.append(process_triple(triple))

    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0].capitalize() + "."
    elif len(sentences) == 2:
        return sentences[0].capitalize() + " and " + sentences[1] + "."
    else:
        result = sentences[0].capitalize()
        for i, sent in enumerate(sentences[1:], 1):
            if i == len(sentences) - 1:
                result += ", and " + sent
            else:
                result += ", " + sent
        return result + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."
